rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery Of Personal Bank Accounts Held At Citibank, N.A., And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Vivian William's Motion for Protective Order Limiting Discovery of Personal Bank Accounts Held at Citibank, N.A., and Request for Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Ms. William's motion is DENIED. Being a trustee is not tied to specific assets of a trust; trustee status relates to the entire trust. A trustee cannot resign as trustee as to some trust assets but remain trustee as to others. Neither party disputes that Brian Williams is a co-trustee of the Trust. Therefore, Brian Williams is a co-trustee as to all trust assets. Even if the resignation as to the Citibank accounts alone was valid, it does not transform Trust bank accounts to accounts owned by Vivian Williams. Furthermore, Ms. Williams alleges that only $50,000 of the decedent's funds were held at Citibank, but the Citibank statements produced by Ms. Williams show that over $950,000 was deposited into the Citibank account. Ms. Williams alleges that most of those funds came from the decedent's other financial institutions, but she has not alleged that Brian Williams resigned as trustee as to assets held in those financial institutions. Ms. Williams also alleges that she comingled her own personal assets with the assets in the Citibank account, but even if this allegation is true, it does not transform a Trust bank account to an account owned by Ms. Williams. The information demanded by the subpoena is the bank account information for two accounts titled in the name of the Williams Living Trust. Evidence Code Section 662 provides: "The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof." The proof presented by Ms. Williams is her declaration that the accounts are hers and a letter signed by Brian which states: "I, Brian D. Williams do hereby relinquish my position as co-trustee of the 1985 Williams Living Trust in regards to any and all Citibank accounts. I authorize Vivian V. Williams to serve as sole trustee regarding any and all Citibank accounts associated with the 1985 Williams Living Trust." The proof offered by Ms. Williams does not satisfy the clear and convincing evidence standard. On this record the Court finds Ms. Williams has not provided sufficient evidence to rebut the presumption that the accounts are trust assets rather than her personal accounts. Ms. Williams does not have an individual right to privacy in Trust accounts against her co-trustee Brian. CCP Section 1985(l) provides: "If the subpoenaing party is the consumer, and the consumer is the only subject of the subpoenaed records, notice to the consumer, and delivery of the other documents specified in subdivision (b) to the consumer, is not required under this section." In this case, the consumer is the Williams Living Trust. Brian Williams and Vivian Williams are co-trustees of the Williams Living Trust. Because as trustee Brian Williams is considered a consumer under California law, he is not required to give notice to himself.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery Of Personal Bank Accounts Held At Citibank, N.A., And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Brian's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Brian Williams has prevailed and sanctions are mandatory unless the court finds that Ms. Williams acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. See CCP Section 2025.420(h). The Court finds that Ms. Williams did NOT act with substantial justification and circumstances do not make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Ms. Williams shall pay to Brian Williams $6,750 within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Ms. Williams's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Brian Williams shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE OSTBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: A person seeking to become a Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify any pleading that supplements or amends his petition(s). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] A petition is either SUPPLEMENTED to provide additional information or AMENDED to provide materially different information and must stand on its own; a "declaration" responding to Examiner's Notes is not a proper supplement (or amendment) to a petition. [CRC 7.3.] If a party consents to electronic service of notice, express consent must be filed. [CCP Section 1010.6(a)(2)(A(ii) and CRC 2.251(b).] No proof of service has been filed yet to evidence correct service of notice to the French consul. Nieces Marjorie Schmidt and Jane Peterson are presumably adults? The proposed executor still has not supplemented Attachment 8 to his petition to set forth the age of these two beneficiaries. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/4/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: continued to 3/2/22. counsel filed a declaration explaining they want the probate court to characterize property notwithstanding the transfer of property by operation of law, but did not provide a legal basis for this relief under the probate code provisions for transfer of property using a spousal property petition. new filing deadline is noon on 2/22/22. counsel may want to provide a robust brief as the judicial officer recently denied a petition seeking the same relief as this one.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SY J. RUSSELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing on close of estate set for 02/27/2023. Bond is not required. Isidoor Bornestein is appointed probate referee.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THELMA L. BOOKER REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/16/2022. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 02/08/2022, even though no courtesy copy of the petition and proposed order had been received. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIE T. HALPIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent's holographic will dated 10/14/2019 named the petitioner as executor and waived his bond. The petitioner signed a proof of holographic instrument as the only person familiar with the decedent's handwriting. The decedent mailed the original holographic will to the petitioner after their telephone conversation wherein the decedent asked the petitioner to hold the original for safekeeping. 2) The petitioner is an attorney who intends to represent himself as executor in this proceeding. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAU CHU TAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 02/27/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 03/13/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHA HENCHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. bond is fixed at $20,000.00. Shirley Yawitz is appointed probate referee. Status hearing on close of estate set for 02/27/2023.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/16/22. Examiner's Notes emailed on 2/3/22. Note: tentative ruling continuances are final and uncontestable and are not subject to Local Rule 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF SHANYA WANASPHITAKSAKUL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. petitioner may be asked whether any specific event prompted the filing of this petition. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number.
